name: Siargao
summary: Siargao is considered one of the safer destinations in the Philippines, with low violent crime rates and a welcoming local community. The island's main risks come from petty theft, tourist-targeted scams, and natural hazards like typhoons during storm season.
quickVerdict: Siargao offers a relatively safe experience for tourists, consistently ranking as one of the Philippines' safer island destinations. While violent crime is rare, visitors should remain alert for petty theft and scams, especially in General Luna's tourist areas. The island's biggest challenges are natural hazards during typhoon season and occasional incidents of tourist harassment. US government employees are permitted to travel to Siargao without special authorization, reflecting its overall safety status compared to other parts of Mindanao.

neighborhoods.name: General Luna
neighborhoods.description: The main tourist hub with the highest concentration of accommodations, restaurants, and nightlife. Generally safe but most prone to petty crime and scams due to heavy tourist traffic.

neighborhoods.name: Cloud 9
neighborhoods.description: Famous surfing area just outside General Luna. Well-monitored and safe during day hours, with good visibility and regular patrol presence.

neighborhoods.name: Dapa Town
neighborhoods.description: The main port town offering a more authentic local experience. Generally safe with lower tourist-targeted crime but fewer amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Pacifico
neighborhoods.description: Remote northern area known for pristine beaches and tranquil atmosphere. Very safe but isolated, requiring careful planning for emergencies.

neighborhoods.name: Del Carmen
neighborhoods.description: Administrative center and gateway town. Safe for tourists but limited attractions and services compared to other areas.

neighborhoods.name: Remote Island Areas
neighborhoods.description: Isolated areas and smaller surrounding islands. Generally safe but limited emergency services, communication, and transport options require extra precautions.

scams.name: Tricycle Fare Overcharging
scams.description: Drivers quote inflated prices to tourists, especially those unfamiliar with local rates, sometimes charging 3-5 times the normal fare.
scams.howToAvoid: Agree on the fare before getting in, ask locals for standard rates, or use the regulated fare chart available in local Facebook groups.

scams.name: Motorbike Rental Damage Claims
scams.description: Rental shops claim pre-existing damage was caused by the renter, demanding payment for scratches, dents, or mechanical issues that existed before rental.
scams.howToAvoid: Take detailed photos and videos of the motorbike before riding, document all existing damage with the rental shop owner, and read rental agreements carefully.

scams.name: Fake Tour Operators
scams.description: Operators advertise island-hopping tours on social media but disappear after payment, or provide substandard services with poor safety equipment.
scams.howToAvoid: Book only with DOT-accredited tour operators, verify credentials, read recent reviews, and avoid paying full amounts upfront through social media.

scams.name: Surfboard Rental Hidden Fees
scams.description: Rental shops charge for alleged damage to surfboards or add unexpected fees for items like leashes or wax that should be included.
scams.howToAvoid: Inspect equipment thoroughly before use, clarify all rental terms and included items upfront, and document the condition of the surfboard with photos.

soloFemale.overview: Siargao is generally considered safe for solo female travelers, with many women reporting positive experiences. The island has a relaxed, backpacker-friendly atmosphere and a growing community of solo travelers. However, some incidents of harassment have been reported, particularly in less crowded areas or at night, and the UK government has noted reports of sexual assault in Siargao.
tips: Stay in well-populated areas like General Luna, especially at night, and avoid isolated beaches or remote areas alone
Join group tours or connect with other travelers for activities in remote locations, as cell service can be limited in isolated areas
Be cautious about drinks and belongings in nightlife areas, as there have been isolated incidents of drink spiking and theft
Use tricycles or organized transport rather than walking alone after dark, and always let someone know your plans
Trust your instincts and don't hesitate to seek help from other tourists, hostel staff, or local authorities if you feel uncomfortable

nightSafety.overview: Siargao's nightlife is generally safe, centered in General Luna with a laid-back beach bar atmosphere. Most venues close by midnight due to local regulations, and the scene is more relaxed compared to major party destinations. The island has good lighting in main areas and tricycles provide safe late-night transport.
tips: Stay in the main General Luna area where most bars and restaurants are concentrated and well-lit
Use tricycles or habal-habal for transportation at night rather than walking alone, especially to remote accommodations
Keep an eye on your drinks and belongings in nightlife venues, as petty theft can occur in crowded areas
Stick to established bars and avoid accepting drinks from strangers or leaving drinks unattended
Travel in groups when possible and let your accommodation know your expected return time

transport.metro: No metro system exists on Siargao. Public transportation consists mainly of tricycles, jeepneys, and multicabs connecting different parts of the island.
transport.rideshare: Grab and other ride-hailing apps are not available on Siargao. Transportation relies on tricycles, habal-habal (motorcycle taxis), and negotiated private transport.
transport.taxis: Traditional taxis are rare on Siargao. Main transport options are tricycles (motorbike with sidecar), habal-habal motorcycle taxis, and rental motorbikes/scooters.
transport.tips: Motorbike rental (PHP 350-600/day) is the most popular and flexible option. Tricycles are safe and readily available but negotiate fares beforehand. Police checkpoints now strictly enforce license requirements - carry an International Driving Permit. Always wear helmets and drive defensively, as accidents are common on the island.

customs: Use polite language including 'po' and 'opo' when speaking with elders or strangers to show respect
Dress modestly when visiting local communities, religious sites, or family gatherings - cover shoulders and knees
Remove shoes when entering homes, and wait to be invited before sitting or eating
Avoid raising your voice or public confrontation, as maintaining social harmony is important in Filipino culture
Show respect for elders through the 'mano po' gesture if offered, and always greet people warmly with a smile

health.overview: Siargao has basic healthcare facilities but limited services for serious emergencies. The nearest major hospital is in Surigao City on the mainland. Medical evacuation insurance is strongly recommended.
health.water: Tap water is not safe for drinking. Use bottled water or properly treated water. Most accommodations provide safe drinking water.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required beyond routine vaccines. Consider hepatitis A/B, typhoid, and Japanese encephalitis for extended rural stays. Dengue and malaria prevention through mosquito bite avoidance is essential.
health.altitude: Siargao is at sea level with a tropical climate. Expect high humidity, strong sun exposure, and seasonal typhoons from June-December. Dehydration and heat exhaustion are common risks.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Manila: +63 2 5301-2000 (24-hour emergency line). Located at 1201 Roxas Boulevard, Manila. Consular services and emergency assistance for US citizens.

faq.a: Yes, Siargao is generally safe for tourists and is considered one of the safer destinations in the Philippines. Violent crime is rare, and the local community is welcoming to visitors. Main concerns are petty theft, tourist scams, and natural hazards during typhoon season.

Siargao is relatively safe at night, especially in the main General Luna area where most nightlife is concentrated. Most venues close by midnight due to local regulations. Use tricycles for transport after dark and stay in well-lit, populated areas.

faq.q: Is Siargao safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Generally yes, but exercise normal precautions. Many solo female travelers visit safely, but there have been some reported incidents of harassment. Stay in populated areas, join group activities for remote locations, and trust your instincts.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Siargao?
faq.a: Avoid isolated beaches and remote areas alone, especially at night. While most of Siargao is safe, areas with limited cell service or emergency access require extra caution. Stay within the main tourist areas of General Luna for maximum safety.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Siargao?
faq.a: Yes, tricycles and jeepneys are generally safe. However, negotiate fares beforehand to avoid overcharging. Motorbike rental is popular but requires an International Driving Permit, helmet use, and defensive driving due to road conditions and traffic patterns.
